title: Quiz: the purpose of a foreign key
prompt_md: What is the main purpose of declaring a FOREIGN KEY (e.g. `bookings.user_id` referencing `users.id`)?
options: To automatically make the column faster to sort
To enforce referential integrity — a booking can't point at a user that doesn't exist
To copy the referenced user's name and email into the bookings table
To make the column store its values in uppercase
explanation_md: A foreign key enforces referential integrity: the database refuses to insert a booking whose `user_id` has no matching `users.id`, and won't let you delete a user who still has bookings (unless configured to cascade). It's a correctness guarantee, not an index or sorting feature, and it stores only the reference (the id) — not a copy of the user's data.
